text.title: Conventional Commits Enforced at the Hook Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of gov-conventional-commits-enforced. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent runs `git commit --no-verify`; non-conformant messages leak to remote.
antipattern.fix: Policy forbids `--no-verify`; audit pre-receive hook on server rejects messages that don't match grammar.

antipattern.symptom: PR squash-merged with title 'wip stuff'; CHANGELOG entry corrupted.
antipattern.root-cause: No CI check on PR title.
antipattern.fix: Add `commitlint` GitHub action gated on PR title.

antipattern.symptom: Agent edits `## [1.4.0]` section; release notes diverge from log.
antipattern.root-cause: No file-permission policy on released sections.
antipattern.fix: Released sections marked read-only by CI lint; only `## [Unreleased]` editable.
